seoTitle: Lamington vs Landsdale — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Lamington and Landsdale. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Lamington and Landsdale.
heading: Lamington vs Landsdale — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Lamington, Western Australia and Landsdale, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Lamington has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Landsdale (35/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Lamington or Landsdale safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Lamington has a flood risk score of 37/100, while Landsdale scores 22/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Lamington scores 36/100 for bushfire risk. Landsdale scores 47/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Lamington or Landsdale?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Lamington has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.68% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
